business.headline: NSE, Jio IPOs have retail investors excited. But are big listings worth the hype?NSE and Jio are already generating plenty of excitement. But before investors join the rush, recent IPO history offers an important lesson about hype, valuations and long-term returns. Read on to find out why big listings don't always translate into big gains.

Will life insurance pay after a fire? Here's what every family should knowRecent fire tragedies have triggered questions over whether life insurance pays after accidental deaths. Experts say standard term plans generally cover such deaths unless false disclosures or policy exclusions apply.

Asian markets crash, will Dalal Street feel the aftershock on Monday?From South Korea's Kospi to Japan's Nikkei, Asian stock markets have tumbled sharply, leaving investors on edge. As Dalal Street prepares to reopen after a three-day holiday on Monday, will the BSE and NSE also open deep in the red?

From ITR filing to TDS: 3 major tax deadlines in July 2026 you shouldn't missWhether you're a salaried employee, a business owner or an employer, keeping track of these dates can help you avoid last-minute stress. Here are three key tax deadlines you should not miss in July 2026.

Will your family get your EPF pension after your death? Here's what you need to knowNo one likes to think about what happens after they're gone. But if you're an EPF member, knowing whether your family can continue receiving your pension could make a big difference to their financial future.

Will DA rise before the 8th Pay Commission? Here's what the latest data suggestsThe wait for the 8th Pay Commission continues, but all eyes are now on the next DA revision. As inflation remains elevated and the July review draws closer, is another DA hike just around the corner?

Govt restores commercial LPG supply to pre-West Asia crisis levelsThe move comes after the government reviewed the country's fuel availability and found that both domestic production and imported LPG supplies have improved.

AI reality check hits Asia as Seoul's Kospi crashes 8%, Tokyo's Nikkei tumbles 5%South Korea's KOSPI fell 8.2%, prompting authorities to halt program trading for 20 minutes after steep losses rippled across the market. Japan's Nikkei 225 dropped 5%, while Hong Kong's Hang Seng declined 2.4%.

What's the one big hurdle delaying India-US trade deal? Piyush Goyal explainsPiyush Goyal said India will bring the US trade deal into force only after Washington creates a tariff framework that favours Indian exporters over regional competitors. The position follows US legal changes that overturned reciprocal tariffs and forced both sides to revisit implementation.

Sebi may allow celebrity endorsements for financial brands. But here's a catchUnder the proposal, celebrities would be allowed to endorse a financial firm's brand or company name, subject to prescribed conditions, disclaimers and prior approvals.

Stock market holiday on Muharram: Are NSE and BSE open for trading on June 26?Planning to trade on Friday, June 26? With Muharram being observed across the country, many investors are wondering whether the NSE and BSE will remain open for trading?

Don't want to pay taxes online? Here's how to use the 'Pay at Bank Counter' optionNot comfortable paying taxes online? You're not alone. The Income Tax Department still allows taxpayers to pay their dues at authorised bank branches through the 'Pay at Bank Counter' facility.

India-US trade deal very close, but here's why it isn't signed yetPiyush Goyal said India and the US are close to finalising their trade deal. India wants tariff terms that give its exporters a clear edge over rivals.

Dalal Street pares gains: Sensex ends flat after climbing over 600 pointsEarly optimism driven by lower crude oil prices and a stronger rupee faded as investors booked profits, though benchmark indices still managed to end in positive territory.

Zerodha's Nithin Kamath has a warning for you against pyramid schemesAccording to figures cited by Kamath, more than 5.5 crore Indians have lost their savings to over 5,300 such schemes. The estimated losses stood at around Rs 10 lakh crore as of 2015, and he suggested that the actual figure could be much higher now.

Gold loses over Rs 51,000 from all-time high. Why is the yellow metal crashing?Gold has fallen sharply from its peak as silver also comes under selling pressure. The correction is being driven by rate-hike bets, a stronger dollar and shifting investor appetite.

United Spirits may cut 100 jobs as Diageo looks to trim costsUnited Spirits is set to lay off around 100 employees as Diageo pursues a global cost-cutting programme. The move comes amid a wider restructuring drive in India despite strong growth and continued investment in premium brands.

Amazon to invest $48 billion in India by 2030, CEO Andy Jassy meets PM ModiAccording to Jassy, the company aims to support 3.8 million jobs in India by 2030 and help generate $80 billion in cumulative e-commerce exports.

Sensex jumps over 600 points: Why are stock markets rallying today?Benchmark indices extended their recovery on Thursday as softer crude, firm Asian cues and gains in banks and autos lifted sentiment. The move has eased near-term macro concerns, though investors remain watchful of the monsoon and global central bank signals.

EPFO portal shutdown from June 26-28: How it will affect your EPF claimsPlanning to withdraw money from your EPF account or submit a claim this week? You may have to postpone it. EPFO has announced a three-day shutdown of key portal services as it upgrades its claims processing system.

entertainment.headline: Suniel Shetty shares granddaughter Evaarah's daily ritual involving PM Modi's photo Suniel Shetty has shared an unusual daily ritual involving his 15-month-old granddaughter Evaarah and a photograph of Prime Minister Narendra Modi. Here's what the actor revealed and why he believes it is significant.

Five-time award-winning cinematographer Dillip Ray dies at 72 after brief illness Cinematographer Dillip Ray has died at 72. Read about his remarkable journey, celebrated films, award-winning career and the tributes pouring in after his death.

Ujjwal Nikam praises Rajkummar Rao for bringing his story to life in Prahaar Public Prosecutor Ujjwal Nikam has praised Rajkummar Rao and Avinash Arun for bringing his story to screen in Prahaar. He said the film captures landmark prosecutions as well as the private loneliness behind them.

Neeraj Ghaywan criticises TVF's Panchayat for featuring 'only upper-caste names' Filmmaker Neeraj Ghaywan has questioned the authenticity of TVF's Panchayat over its portrayal of village life. Here's what he said and why his remarks have sparked discussion.

Jackky Bhagnani's old dating app profile goes viral after situationship remarks Jackky Bhagnani is back in the spotlight after old screenshots of his alleged Raya profile resurfaced on Reddit, months after his "situationship" remark about wife Rakul Preet Singh sparked controversy.

Suniel Shetty on Don 3 row: Ranveer Singh brought Rs 3,500 crore to the industry Actor Suniel Shetty has come out in support of Ranveer Singh amid the Don 3 controversy, questioning calls to ban the actor and making a strong case for artistic freedom. Here's what he said.

Priyadarshan's Haiwaan starring Akshay Kumar and Saif Ali Khan gets release date Akshay Kumar and Saif Ali Khan's much-awaited reunion film Haiwaan has officially locked its theatrical release date. Here's everything revealed so far about Priyadarshan's upcoming thriller.

K Bhagyaraj: The screenplay king who became the voice of middle-class India Veteran filmmaker K Bhagyaraj, celebrated as Tamil cinema's King of Screenplay, died in Chennai on June 27 after a cardiac arrest. His death, coming just days after mentor Bharathiraja's death, marks another major loss for the industry.

K Bhagyaraj funeral: CM Vijay and Rajinikanth arrive to honour the filmmaker Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay and superstar Rajinikanth arrived to pay their final respects to veteran filmmaker K Bhagyaraj. The legendary director, who died at 73, is being accorded state honours for his immense contribution to Tamil cinema.

K Bhagyaraj death: Tamil Nadu announces state honours for legendary filmmaker The Tamil Nadu government, under Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay, announced state honours for veteran filmmaker K Bhagyaraj following his death at 73. Tributes poured in from across the film fraternity, with Venkatesh Daggubati, Kamal Haasan, MK Stalin, Raghava Lawrence, Selvaraghavan and Simran remembering his lasting legacy.

Mohanlal makes Germany dance to his tunes, grooves to Dhanush's Megham Karukatha Mohanlal turned a star-studded event in Germany into a memorable celebration as he sang and danced to popular tracks, including Dhanush's Megham Karukatha, Illuminati and Kalapakkara. Videos of the Malayalam superstar's energetic stage performance with Saniya Iyappan and KS Chithra have gone viral online.

The trailer trap and forgetting art of surprise: How much is too much? What makes a great movie trailer? Audiences, editors, filmmakers and trade experts discuss the fine balance between building curiosity and giving away too much.

Samantha's Maa Inti Bangaaram sequel is on, confirms writer Raj Nidimoru Samantha Ruth Prabhu's Maa Inti Bangaaram is set to return with a sequel, writer Raj Nidimoru announced during the film's success meet in Vizag. The announcement comes after the Nandini Reddy directorial emerged as the highest-grossing women-led film and received widespread praise.

Veteran Tamil filmmaker, writer K Bhagyaraj dies of heart attack at 73 Veteran Tamil filmmaker, actor and screenwriter K Bhagyaraj died in Chennai at the age of 73 following a heart attack. His funeral is scheduled to be held at 1.30 pm at Chennai's Besant Nagar crematorium.

Cocktail 2 box office Day 8: Shahid film on track to surpass Teri Baaton Mein… Cocktail 2 has crossed Rs 114.67 crore at the worldwide box office after eight days, while its India nett collection has reached Rs 74.75 crore. Shahid Kapoor and Kriti Sanon's romantic drama is now closing in on the lifetime domestic earnings of Teri Baaton Mein Aisa Uljha Jiya.

Welcome To The Jungle box office Day 1: Akshay film scores big, beats Bhooth Bangla Akshay Kumar's Welcome To The Jungle has registered a decent opening at the box office, collecting Rs 29 crore worldwide on its first day despite mixed reviews. The comedy entertainer also surpassed the opening-day collections of the actor's recent films Bhooth Bangla and Jolly LLB 3.

Millie Bobby Brown-David Harbour reunite for Adolescence creator's spy series Actors Millie Bobby Brown and David Harbour will reunite in an untitled spy drama from Jack Thorne. The series casts them as estranged father and daughter at the centre of a mission crisis.

Akshay Kumar says Anil Kapoor-Nana Patekar are family, hints at Welcome 4 return Actor Akshay Kumar said actors Anil Kapoor and Nana Patekar remain part of the Welcome family despite missing Welcome To The Jungle. His remarks keep the door open for a reunion in a fourth film.

Rahul Ravindran defends Rashmika's The Girlfriend amid Pune murder case row Director Rahul Ravindran responded after social media users linked The Girlfriend to the Ketan Agarwal murder case. His post drew a distinction between individual crimes and the wider gender imbalance his film addresses.

general news.headline: Have India and Pakistan started fresh track-2 diplomacy? Here's what we knowThere are reports claiming that India and Pakistan held a track-2 dialogue on the sidelines of a security conference held in Sri Lanka this week. Some reports are referring to it as track-1.5 dialogue, claiming that serving officials were involved. Did representatives from India and Pakistan hold talks? Here's what we know.

Magnitude 6.2 earthquake jolts Afghanistan, tremors felt in India, PakTremors from the deep-focus Afghanistan earthquake were felt across Pakistan, northern India and Central Asia, while authorities reported no immediate casualties or widespread damage.

Any exam left? Opposition mounts criticism as paper leak hits Maharashtra TETThe Maharashtra TET was postponed about 20 hours before the exam after parts of the question paper were allegedly leaked. Opposition parties used the development to intensify attacks on the BJP after the massive fiasco following the NEET-UG paper leak.

Mumbai cops uncover Muharram poisoning plot, seize 14,900 rat poison capsulesMumbai Police detained a Pune businessman after seizing 14,900 capsules allegedly filled with zinc phosphide during a Muharram procession. Investigators are now examining his travels, finances and contacts for possible wider links.

India now dominates global research paper retractions with 6 universities in top 10India's rise in global research rankings is now shadowed by a sharp spike in paper retractions. With six Indian universities among the world's top 10 for retractions, concerns around plagiarism, fake peer reviews, and flawed incentives are growing, raising questions about credibility, funding, and the future of Indian science.

Kill Ketan, buy time: Siya Goyal wanted 3 more years before marriage, say copsPune police have revealed that Siya Goyal believed fiancé Ketan Agarwal's death would delay marriage plans by at least three years, while co-accused Chetan Chaudhary also wanted more time before settling down. This came as a new twist to the murder case.

Lashkar terrorists, its deputy chief attend funeral of Shoaib Akhtar's brotherA viral video shows Saifullah Kasuri, deputy chief of the Lashkar-e-Taiba, and other terrorists at Shahid Akhtar's funeral in Islamabad. Shahid, the elder brother of Pakistani great Shoaib Akhtar, passed away earlier this week.

Lot of similarities between PM Modi, Trump: US envoy decodes both leadersUS Ambassador Sergio Gor said Narendra Modi and Donald Trump shared a close bond despite recent tensions in India-US ties. Gor said their hands-on style and personal chemistry would steady the relationship.

India makes hydrogen from nuclear heat, not electricity. Why scientists are thrilledIndia has inaugurated the world's first plant that makes hydrogen using nuclear reactor heat instead of electricity. Built at Kalpakkam, the copper-chlorine cycle points to a cleaner, round-the-clock route to green hydrogen.

Sooryavanshi winked at me, promised 13 sixes: Sangakkara reveals stunning IPL storyKumar Sangakkara recalled Vaibhav Sooryavanshi winking and predicting 13 sixes before a crucial IPL chase. The anecdote captured the 15-year-old's unusual self-belief as he moves closer to an India debut.

One Sunday, two extremes: Heavy rain to hit Assam, heat to grip Uttar PradeshAssam and Meghalaya are set for extremely heavy rain on Sunday while Uttar Pradesh remains under heat wave conditions. The split weather pattern is being driven by the monsoon trough, cyclonic circulations and a delayed monsoon over the northern plains.

I'm a farmer: Union minister defends Rs 1 crore subsidy for farm from his ministryUnion minister Bhagirath Choudhary added that setting up a polyhouse without government assistance would not be financially viable for an ordinary farmer, which is why such subsidies are provided.

From 1% hope to history: Cape Verde's World Cup dream refuses to endCape Verde, the side representing an archipelago of 10 islands in the Atlantic Ocean, arrived at the World Cup with just a one per cent chance of reaching the knockout stage. Three disciplined performances later, the Blue Sharks had become the smallest nation ever to reach the World Cup knockout rounds.

PM Modi's birthday wish for Education Minister pours cold water on exit calls?Prime Minister Narendra Modi praised Dharmendra Pradhan on his birthday amid demands for the Education Minister's removal over the NEET-UG paper leak row. The message is being read as a sign of PM's backing as protests grow and amid Cabinet reshuffle buzz.

Noida Authority gets new administrative hub as CM Yogi inaugurates Rs 390-crore HQChief Minister Yogi Adityanath inaugurated the Noida Authority's new Rs 390-crore headquarters in Sector 96 on Saturday. The long-delayed campus brings key departments together and is expected to improve public services.

Live: PM Modi visits National Botanical Garden with Seychelles President HerminiePM Modi Seychelles visit Live Updates: Prime Minister Narendra Modi left for a three-day visit to Seychelles on Saturday, where he will attend the island nation's National Day golden jubilee celebrations and hold bilateral talks with President Patrick Herminie to further strengthen ties between the two countries. PM Modi, in his departure statement, said that the visit undertaken at the invitation of President Herminie, coincides with the 50th anniversary of diplomatic relations between India and Seychelles. During the trip, PM Modi will also become the first Indian Prime Minister to address the Seychelles National Assembly. Follow IndiaToday.in for all the latest updates on PM Modi's Seychelles visit.

K Bhagyaraj: The screenplay king who became the voice of middle-class IndiaVeteran filmmaker K Bhagyaraj, celebrated as Tamil cinema's King of Screenplay, died in Chennai on June 27 after a cardiac arrest. His death, coming just days after mentor Bharathiraja's death, marks another major loss for the industry.
Question Time: Weekly News QuizThink you read the headlines this week? Let's test that!

Fresh tanker attack rocks Hormuz amid worst Iran-US escalation since truceThe warring sides each accused the other of violating the agreement they reached two weeks ago to end the four-month-old conflict. Washington said it hit Iranian targets overnight, while Iran said it had struck targets linked to US forces on Saturday in response.

When Sikhs debate beadbi, what is defining it theologically — Sikhi or Sharia?The Sikh debate over 'beadbi' (sacrilege) has consumed the headlines. Beneath it sits a harder question: when a community's historical words are replaced one by one, without discussion or sanction, and the replacements keep pointing in an alien direction, what do we call that?

The trailer trap and forgetting art of surprise: How much is too much?What makes a great movie trailer? Audiences, editors, filmmakers and trade experts discuss the fine balance between building curiosity and giving away too much.
Engine trouble: Why European engine firms are making a beeline to IndiaA Franco-British contest is underway for India's future jet fighters.

NSE, Jio IPOs have retail investors excited. But are big listings worth the hype?NSE and Jio are already generating plenty of excitement. But before investors join the rush, recent IPO history offers an important lesson about hype, valuations and long-term returns. Read on to find out why big listings don't always translate into big gains.

Maharashtra Teachers Eligibility Test postponed after paper leak day before examThe Maharashtra Teacher Eligibility Test (TET 2026), scheduled for June 28, has been postponed after concerns over a possible question paper leak surfaced in Thane district. The decision follows a police raid in Bhiwandi and preliminary findings linking recovered questions to the exam paper.

sports.headline: Ancy Sojan breaks Anju Bobby George's long jump national record Ancy Sojan shattered Anju Bobby George's 22-year-old women's long jump national record with a historic 6.88m leap at the National Inter State Senior Athletics Championships on Saturday.

Sarvesh Kushare smashes high jump national record, books Asian Games berth Sarvesh Kushare set a new men's high jump national record by clearing 2.31m to win gold at the National Inter-State Athletics Championships in Bhubaneswar, eclipsing Tejaswin Shankar's eight-year-old mark of 2.29m.

India under pressure, Australia ready to make their life tougher: Sophie Molineux Women's T20 World Cup, INDW vs AUSW: Australia captain Sophie Molineux said her side will look to capitalise on the pressure India are under in Sunday's crucial Group A clash at Lord's Cricket Ground.

No money for equipment, Sindhushree breaks national record with borrowed pole G Sindhushree broke the national pole vault record with a borrowed pole to win gold and seal an Asian Games berth. She dedicated the achievement to her late father, who had backed her sporting journey.

India will draw confidence vs Australia from recent T20I series win: Shafali Verma Women's T20 World Cup, INDW vs AUSW: Shafali Verma believes India will draw confidence from their recent 2-1 T20I series victory over Australia on Australian soil going into the Lord's clash.

Sooryavanshi winked at me, promised 13 sixes: Sangakkara reveals stunning IPL story Kumar Sangakkara recalled Vaibhav Sooryavanshi winking and predicting 13 sixes before a crucial IPL chase. The anecdote captured the 15-year-old's unusual self-belief as he moves closer to an India debut.

Naomi Osaka suffers injury scare before Wimbledon, retires from Bad Homburg final Naomi Osaka's Wimbledon preparations suffered a major setback after she retired injured from the Bad Homburg Open final, handing Karolina Muchova the title just two days before the Grand Slam begins.

No one helps us: Furious Iran captain Taremi brands FIFA World Cup a 'disaster' Iran captain Mehdi Taremi tore into FIFA and its president, Gianni Infantino, after his side's World Cup campaign was hampered by travel restrictions and logistical problems, saying the tournament had been a "disaster" for Iran.

Badminton: Rounak Chouhan continues dream run, reaches semifinals of US Open Qualifier Rounak Chouhan beat Misha Zilberman to enter the US Open 2026 semi-finals. His surge followed a straight-games upset of top seed Chou Tien Chen, raising prospects of an all-Indian final.

From 1% hope to history: Cape Verde's World Cup dream refuses to endCape Verde, the side representing an archipelago of 10 islands in the Atlantic Ocean, arrived at the World Cup with just a one per cent chance of reaching the knockout stage. Three disciplined performances later, the Blue Sharks had become the smallest nation ever to reach the World Cup knockout rounds.

Missing hurdle: Teen athlete forced to run solo after shocking blunder from officialsInter-state Athletics Championships 2026: A shocking track blunder saw technical officials place only nine hurdles instead of 10, cruelly forcing Tamil Nadu sprinter Harshitha into a gruelling, lonely solo re-run that ultimately ended her hopes of making it to the final of the 400m hurdles event.

Samson's spot under threat? Sooryavanshi breathing down his neck, warns ManjrekarIndia tour of Ireland: Sanjay Manjrekar warns Sanju Samson to be careful as 15-year-old prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi piles on the pressure following India's historic and shocking T20I defeat against Ireland in the Belfast series opener.

Harshit Rana shrugs off painful past to deliver when India needed him mostIndia vs Ireland, 1st T20I: Harshit Rana impressed on his return to the Indian side, claiming career-best T20I figures of 3 for 24, but his efforts went in vain as Ireland defeated India by 34 runs on Friday.

Belgium thrash NZ to reach knockouts, Iran face anxious wait after Egypt drawBelgium thrashed New Zealand 5-1 to finish top of Group G, while Egypt held Iran to a 1-1 draw in Seattle. The results sent Belgium and Egypt into the FIFA World Cup Round of 32, while Iran were left waiting to discover if they will qualify as one of the tournament's eight best third-placed teams.

Small islands, wild joy: Watch Cape Verde celebrate historic qualification in HoustonFIFA World Cup 2026: Cape Verde reached the World Cup knockouts after drawing Saudi Arabia and waiting for Spain to beat Uruguay. The debutants became the smallest nation ever to do so and will now face Argentina in Round of 32.

Marcelo Bielsa shouts at reporter, accepts blame for Uruguay's World Cup exitFIFA World Cup: Uruguay manager Marcelo Bielsa shouted at a reporter during his post-match press conference after his side's group-stage exit on Friday before accepting full responsibility for the team's disappointing campaign.

Highlights: Belgium and Egypt go through, Iran face anxious waitFIFA World Cup 2026 Highlights, Egypt vs Iran and Belgium vs New Zealand: A frenetic final matchday in Group G ends. Belgium are through to the Round of 32 as the group winners. They hammered New Zealand 5-1. Egypt and Iran played out a 1-1 draw in Seattle. Egypt are through and Iran need to wait until Sunday afternoon to learn their fate.

Cape Verde seal dream knockout berth, Uruguay crash out after Spain defeatFIFA World Cup: Uruguay's campaign came to a disappointing end on Friday after a 1-0 defeat to Spain, while tournament debutants Cape Verde completed a remarkable journey into the Round of 32 with a goalless draw against Saudi Arabia.

How India can qualify for Women's T20 World Cup semi-finals: Scenarios explainedWomen's T20 World Cup: Harmanpreet Kaur's India have it all to play for against Australia in their final Group A clash, with a place in the semi-finals still up for grabs and the qualification race set to go down to the final day of the group stage.

The Jai Moondra story: How a Master's degree scripted dream Ireland debut vs IndiaIND vs IRE: Jai Moondra moved to Ireland in 2021 to pursue a Master's degree in Electronics and Communication. Five years later, the Indian-born cricketer realised his dream of playing international cricket by representing his adopted nation.

Lionel Messi to be benched for Argentina's final group-stage game vs JordanLionel Messi will start on the bench against Jordan as Argentina coach Lionel Scaloni rotates his squad after sealing top spot in Group J. Messi is expected to feature later in the World Cup clash.

Reece James out, Saka fit: Tuchel reveals England team news before Panama clashEngland defender Reece James has been ruled out of the crucial World Cup Group L clash against Panama with a hamstring injury. Thomas Tuchel confirmed Bukayo Saka, Declan Rice and Elliot Anderson are fit.

URU 0-1 ESP HIGHLIGHTS: Spain, Cabo Verde into knockouts, Uruguay crash outUruguay (URU) vs Spain (ESP) Live, FIFA World Cup 2026: (URU 0-1 ESP) Group H has exploded into life at the break. Spain have one foot in the knockouts after capitalising on a costly Muslera error, while Cape Verde are just 45 minutes away from a historic last-32 berth. As things stand, Uruguay are heading for a stunning World Cup exit.

Dembele steals the Mbappe vs Haaland show as France breeze past NorwayFIFA World Cup 2026: France marched into the Round of 32 with a perfect Group I record after Ousmane Dembele's stunning first-half hat-trick inspired a 4-1 win over Norway. Elsewhere, Senegal crushed Iraq 5-0 to keep their hopes alive of progressing as one of the World Cup's best third-placed teams.

Senegal's historic five-goal blitz breathes life into World Cup campaignSenegal vs Iraq, FIFA World Cup 2026: Senegal thrashed 10-man Iraq 5-0 to keep their FIFA World Cup knockout hopes alive, becoming the first African nation to score five goals in a men's World Cup match.

Ousmane Dembele silences doubters with historic World Cup hat-trick for FranceOusmane Dembele's historic World Cup hat-trick against Norway marked the defining moment of his France career, capping years of perseverance with a record-breaking performance.

India hammer Pakistan 7-1 in FIH Pro League, extend decade-long dominanceIndia produced a commanding comeback to thrash Pakistan 7-1 in the FIH Pro League after conceding an early penalty-corner goal, with Harmanpreet Singh leading a clinical attacking display. The emphatic victory puts India seventh on the points table, above Spain and Pakistan.

Jemimah tells India to go on the attack against AustraliaWomen's T20 World Cup, INDW vs AUSW: Jemimah Rodrigues said that India need to be aggressive and can't afford to be defensive when they face Australia at the Lord's Cricket Ground on Sunday.

FIFA World Cup Highlights: France crush Norway, Senagal alive after 5-0 Iraq winNorway vs France Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: France marched into the knockouts with a commanding 4-1 win over Norway, finishing Group I with a perfect record. Elsewhere, Senegal kept their World Cup dream alive by thrashing Iraq 5-0 to boost their hopes of sneaking through as one of the eight best third-placed teams.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
